title: Add herdr-go service {start|stop|restart|status} subcommand
action: Add a `service` subcommand to herdr-go's hand-written arg parser: `herdr-go service {start|stop|restart|status}` (per D2, D3). Extend the runtime-probe pattern already used by active_service_restart() in src/doctor/checks.rs (per D5) to cover all three platforms (per D4) and all four verbs: Linux via `systemctl --user <verb> herdr-go.service` (probe with `systemctl --user is-active`), macOS via launchctl (kickstart -k for restart, bootstrap/bootout/print as appropriate for start/stop/status, matching install.sh's existing invocations at install.sh:195-213), Windows via the Scheduled Task equivalents used in install.ps1 (Start-ScheduledTask/Stop-ScheduledTask/Get-ScheduledTask at install.ps1:60-62,146-171, invoked via a shelled-out command since no native Windows service-control bindings exist in this crate). Each verb is a thin pass-through: run the native command and surface its own exit status/stdout/stderr as-is, no extra idempotency guard logic (per D7). When no service manager is detected (probe returns None on every platform branch), print a clear error to stderr naming the failure and pointing at the manual docs, then exit non-zero -- same shape as the existing `unknown argument` error convention in parse_args() at src/main.rs:75-79 (per D6). Add a `service` line to print_help()'s COMMANDS section describing the new subcommand and its four verbs.

truths: herdr-go service start|stop|restart|status are all recognized by parse_args() and no longer fall into the unknown argument branch
On this dev machine (Linux), herdr-go service status reports the herdr-go.service systemd --user unit's actual state via systemctl --user is-active
When no service manager is detected, the command prints a stderr error naming the failure and exits non-zero instead of silently no-op'ing

artifacts.substantive: parse_args() recognizes service + 4 verbs; print_help() COMMANDS section documents service
platform-probe logic extended/reused for all 3 platforms and 4 verbs, not restart-only

key_links: main.rs's new service branch calls into the extended checks.rs probe/action logic
prohibitions: No new crate dependency (e.g. no clap)
No change to the existing doctor subcommand's own behavior
No extra idempotency/state-guard logic layered on top of the native OS commands (per D7)

verify: cargo build --quiet && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo run --quiet -- service status

trace.outcome: Added herdr-go service {start|stop|restart|status}: runtime-probes systemd --user (Linux), launchd (macOS), and a shelled-out PowerShell Scheduled Task call (Windows), thin pass-through to the native command's own exit status/output, stderr error + exit 2 when no manager is found.
deviations: Added `pub use checks::run_service_command;` to src/doctor/mod.rs (not in the cell's declared files list [src/main.rs, src/doctor/checks.rs]). checks.rs's `mod checks;` is private, so main.rs (a separate binary crate consuming herdr_go::) could not otherwise reach the new function -- required plumbing for the feature to compile and link, same pattern already used for `pub use checks::Check;` on the line above it.

  "tests_inspected": ["src/main.rs main_migration_seam_obeys_the_cli_mode_matrix test", "src/doctor/checks.rs unit tests"],

  "red_failure_evidence": "Before this change, `cargo run -- service status` (and start/stop/restart) fell into parse_args()'s generic unmatched-token branch: it printed 'unknown argument: service' to stderr, printed the full help text, and exited with code 2 -- confirmed by `git stash`-ing this change and re-running the exact command against the prior committed src/main.rs (commit 9863996), which reproduced that output verbatim.",
  "verification_run": "cargo build --quiet && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo run --quiet -- service status -> clean build, clean clippy, clean fmt, and service status printed the real `systemctl --user show herdr-go.service --property=...` output (LoadState=not-found, ActiveState=inactive, SubState=dead -- the unit is not installed on this dev machine, correctly reported) and exited 0. Also manually exercised service start/stop/restart argument parsing, --help (now lists `service`), `service foo` (bad verb -> exit 2, same convention as unknown argument), and `service` with no verb (-> exit 2). Full `cargo test --quiet` suite: 271+2+3 passed, 0 failed -- no regressions.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["macOS (launchd) and Windows (Scheduled Task) verb invocations are not exercised on this Linux dev machine -- only the Linux systemd branch is verified live. The macOS/Windows commands are constructed to exactly match install.sh's launchctl invocations (kickstart -k, print <target>, already used unchanged by active_service_restart()) and install.ps1's PowerShell cmdlets (Start-ScheduledTask/Stop-ScheduledTask/Get-ScheduledTask, D7's own cited references), so correctness rests on matching those known-working scripts rather than a live run."]
}
